data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube's architecture is built on a microservices approach, allowing independent deployment and scaling of services.
data.technical_spec: Multiple services run independently, connected through APIs, promoting scalability and flexibility.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Utilizes a global CDN to efficiently deliver video content to users worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Edge servers positioned globally cache content for quick delivery.

data.title: Distributed Database Systems
data.description: Utilizes distributed databases to handle vast amounts of user and video data, ensuring quick access and resilience.
data.technical_spec: Employs NoSQL databases for scalability and fault tolerance.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Implements load balancing across servers to optimize resource use and provide high availability.
data.technical_spec: Load balancers distribute incoming traffic to maintain service reliability.

data.title: Data Replication and Sharding
data.description: Employs data replication and sharding techniques to ensure data availability and partition tolerance.
data.technical_spec: Data is divided into shards, replicated across multiple servers for redundancy.
